summary: Comprehensive forensic legal analysis examining whether Wincham Group was legally permitted to operate fiscal representation and property advisory services in Lanzarote (Canary Islands) without any registered office or physical presence. Covers four interlocking legal frameworks: Spanish national law (LGT art.47, IRNR art.10, Ley 17/2009), Canarian REF special regime (Ley 19/1994), IGIC obligations (ATC), and the post-Brexit third-country reclassification that stripped Wincham of EU free-movement privileges from 1 January 2021. Identifies 6 specific legal violations with statutory citations.
